name: Peppered Pear-Raspberry Salsa
recipeIngredient: 1 pint fresh raspberries, halved into 1/2-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the pear pieces in a small, deep, preferably glass bowl.
2. Add the minced serrano peppers, sweet white wine, raspberry vinegar, and lime juice to the pear mixture.
3. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
4. After chilling, drain any remaining liquid and discard the chiles and marinade.
5. Reserve 2 tablespoons of the leftover liquid from step 3.
6. Add the halved raspberries to the pears.
7. In a small bowl, combine the reserved liquid, grated ginger, and crushed red pepper flakes.
8. Pour the mixture over the fruit and gently stir to coat evenly.
9. Serve immediately or cover tightly and refrigerate for no more than 2 hours before serving. This salsa is best enjoyed fresh.
